title: kepa fistulosa
intro: Kepa fistulosa, also known as the Kepa plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae that is native to New Zealand. It is traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health conditions, such as respiratory issues, skin problems, and digestive issues, offering potential health benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the Kepa plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
